title: Lastrea blanda
intro: Lastrea blanda, also known as the common polypody or golden polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in temperate and subtropical regions worldwide. The health benefits of Lastrea blanda include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lastrea blanda is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and dried extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lastrea blanda is a species of fern in the genus Lastrea. It is a member of the family Blechnaceae, which is a group of ferns that are commonly known as the chain ferns. Lastrea blanda is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and is often found growing in humid environments. The classification of Lastrea blanda is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the genus Lastrea, Lastrea blanda is closely related to other species of ferns in the same genus.
